title: <b>ФОРМУВАННЯ ІНСТИТУЦІЙНОЇ МОДЕЛІ ЗАБЕЗПЕЧЕННЯ ІНФОРМАЦІЙНОЇ БЕЗПЕКИ БІЗНЕСУ В РЕГІОНІ</b>
abstract: The article provides a comprehensive analysis of the challenges related to ensuring business information security under conditions of digital transformation and intensifying cyber threats, which is particularly relevant for regional economic systems characterized by uneven levels of digital maturity and limited resources for implementing modern protection mechanisms. The key factors hindering the development of effective information security systems are identified, including the fragmentation of existing practices, insufficient formalization of security policies, the absence of structured incident-response procedures, low levels of digital literacy among employees, and the weak institutionalization of cybersecurity processes. Based on an analysis of scientific approaches, regulatory frameworks, and current practical solutions, the study substantiates the relevance of applying an institutional approach to form a holistic model of business information security at the regional level. As a result of the research, an integrated institutional model has been developed, comprising managerial-organizational, regulatory-policy, communication, educational, and technical-technological components that are logically and functionally interconnected. The model ensures a clear distribution of responsibilities, the introduction of transparent information security policies, the establishment of internal communication channels, the enhancement of employees’ digital competencies, and the implementation of accessible technological cybersecurity tools. It has been demonstrated that such a model contributes to the formation of a corporate security culture, increases organizational resilience to cyber incidents, reduces system vulnerabilities, and supports regional economic development by strengthening the security of the business environment. The findings of the study may be applied to improving regional information security policies, developing support programs for small and medium-sized enterprises, and adapting corporate cybersecurity strategies to the conditions of wartime challenges and post-war recovery.

website: Sekolah Tinggi Informatika dan Komputer Indonesia
title: Development of a Teacher Administrative Information System Digitally-Based Inside the Classroom
abstract: The development of information technology encourages educational institutions to undergo digital transformation in administrative management, including student attendance recording. MA Syamsul Jinan is still using a manual attendance system that causes various problems, such as low efficiency, a high potential for recording errors, difficulties in data storage, and the risk of attendance data manipulation. This study aims to develop a web-based digital attendance system as part of a classroom administrative information system to improve the efficiency, accuracy, and transparency of managing student attendance data. The research method used is Research and Development (R&D) with the Waterfall development model which includes requirements analysis, design, implementation, testing, and maintenance. Data were collected through observation, questionnaires, and documentation. System quality evaluation refers to the ISO/IEC 25010 standard with a focus on six aspects, namely functionality suitability, usability, reliability, user satisfaction, maintainability, and portability. The results show that all system features function well, with a usability level of 87.6% which falls into the very good category. The system has reliability, ease of maintenance, and can run optimally on various devices and browsers. Thus, the web-based digital attendance system is declared feasible to implement and capable of improving the quality of educational administration at MA Syamsul Jinan.

title: Information‐Driven Modeling of Energy Markets: An Unbalanced Wasserstein Barycenter Approach
abstract: ABSTRACT A novel methodology is proposed for jointly modeling the price dynamics of natural gas and electricity by integrating graph‐based Machine Learning and optimal transport theory. The framework combines visibility graph embeddings with the Wasserstein barycenter to uncover latent structures and asymmetric dependencies between the two interconnected energy markets. Log‐return time series are first transformed into visibility graphs and then embedded into high‐dimensional vector spaces, where complex temporal and structural patterns become more discernible. In the embedding space, an information‐driven Wasserstein barycenter is computed by optimizing the barycenter weights via Shannon entropy maximization. This procedure reveals an asymmetric balance between the two markets, with natural gas exerting a structurally dominant influence. To characterize the joint stochastic dynamics, a Gaussian Mixture Model is fitted to the thus determined unbalanced Wasserstein barycenter using maximum likelihood estimation via the Expectation–Maximization algorithm. An additional Gaussian component is introduced for each commodity to capture market‐specific behavior. The resulting model can be calibrated to match the first four moments of the empirical log‐return distributions and their observed correlation. Applied to Italian market data from 2019 to 2023, a period marked by extreme volatility and systemic shocks, the methodology accurately reproduces both common dynamics and idiosyncratic deviations. The analysis reveals that the entropy‐optimal barycentric weights are for natural gas and for electricity, highlighting a dominant role of the natural gas market in the joint representation. Compared with a comprehensive benchmark of GARCH‐type models, the proposed framework exhibits markedly superior empirical performance. The approach provides a robust, interpretable, and adaptable tool for risk analysis, derivative pricing, and the study of structural interactions across energy markets.

title: UTAUT Antecedents Shaping Institutional Investors’ Intentions to Utilize ESG Information
abstract: This study examines how institutional investors adopt and utilize Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) information by integrating the Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology (UTAUT). Using the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) with expert-based pairwise comparisons from 20 senior investment professionals at major South Korean financial institutions, we identify and weight key determinants influencing ESG information use among South Korean institutional investors. The results show that performance expectancy emerged as the most influential determinant (33.7%), followed by facilitating conditions (24.6%), social influence (22.8%), and effort expectancy (18.9%). At the sub-criterion level, usefulness for investment decision-making (11.2%), institutional encouragement (10.2%), and utilization of ESG information as a fiduciary duty (9.4%) recorded the highest global weights, whereas psychological comfort in utilizing ESG information (2.0%) and practical guidelines and training programs (3.7%) exhibited the lowest. These findings suggest that ESG adoption has evolved beyond early legitimacy-seeking behavior toward substantive and performance-driven integration, consistent with UTAUT predictions that performance expectancy and facilitating conditions gain salience in mature adoption phases, while effort expectancy and social influence diminish. This weight distribution indicates that ESG has been internalized as core analytical infrastructure informing investment decision-making and risk management, rather than functioning as a peripheral compliance tool. By empirically mapping ESG adoption determinants into a hierarchical structure, this study contributes to the literature on ESG diffusion, institutional investor behavior, and adoption theory, offering practical implications for regulators and financial institutions seeking to deepen substantive ESG integration.

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: Transformación Digital e Inteligencia Artificial como Agregadores de Valor en la Industria Financiera: Una Revisión Sistemática de la Literatura
abstract: La investigación tiene como objetivo, analizar cómo la transformación digital y la inteligencia artificial contribuyen a la generación de valor en la industria financiera, con especial énfasis en el sector bancario. Se utilizó un enfoque cualitativo con diseño descriptivo, a través de este enfoque se identificaron las principales tendencias, aportes y vacíos en la literatura científica, ofreciendo una visión integral del fenómeno. La información se obtuvo mediante una búsqueda sistemática en bases de datos académicas reconocidas; como Scopus, Web of Science, ProQuest, Scielo y Dialnet. Los resultados muestran que la transformación digital y la inteligencia artificial se han convertido en los principales motores de cambio y generación de valor dentro de la industria financiera. La transformación digital ha impulsado la modernización estructural de las organizaciones, promoviendo la automatización de procesos, la reducción de costos y la creación de nuevos modelos de negocio basados en la innovación y la experiencia del cliente. Este proceso no solo implica la adopción de herramientas tecnológicas, sino también una profunda reconfiguración cultural orientada a la agilidad, la eficiencia y la sostenibilidad. Por su parte, la inteligencia artificial complementa esta evolución al optimizar la toma de decisiones mediante el análisis predictivo, la personalización de servicios y la gestión avanzada del riesgo. Su implementación ha permitido mejorar la productividad, fortalecer la seguridad y aumentar la satisfacción del cliente mediante soluciones inteligentes que anticipan necesidades y comportamientos. Sin embargo, también emergen desafíos éticos y regulatorios relacionados con la privacidad de los datos y la transparencia de los algoritmos.

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: La Inteligencia Artificial en la Transformación de la Educación Militar
abstract: En un mundo caracterizado por la acelerada evolución tecnológica y los constantes desafíos geopolíticos, las fuerzas armadas enfrentan la necesidad de adaptar sus modelos formativos a las exigencias del siglo XXI. En este contexto, la inteligencia artificial (IA) emerge como una herramienta clave para transformar la educación militar, no solo desde una perspectiva técnica, sino también pedagógica y estratégica. Esta transformación implica repensar la forma en que se adquieren, procesan y aplican conocimientos y habilidades en contextos operativos complejos. La integración de la IA en los procesos formativos militares permite personalizar el aprendizaje, optimizar los entrenamientos mediante simulaciones avanzadas, y facilitar la toma de decisiones basadas en análisis predictivos. Complementariamente, la Neurodidáctica disciplina que une los aportes de la neurociencia y la educación proporciona una base científica para comprender cómo aprende el cerebro humano bajo condiciones de presión, peligro o incertidumbre, características propias del ámbito militar. Este trabajo explora el impacto de la inteligencia artificial en la educación militar, abordando sus aplicaciones actuales, beneficios, desafíos éticos y proyecciones futuras. A través del análisis del estado del arte y de estudios recientes, se busca comprender cómo la IA está redefiniendo la formación de líderes militares, promoviendo una enseñanza más eficiente, adaptativa y alineada con las demandas operativas contemporáneas.

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: Calidad de Información de Respuestas de las Herramientas de Inteligencia Artificial Generativa Tipo Texto
abstract: Actualmente, las herramientas de inteligencia artificial generativa tipo texto (IAG) se utilizan en educación superior, la problemática que se aborda es sobre calidad de información que éstas generan. El objetivo es evaluar la calidad de las respuestas generadas por herramientas IAG a diferentes prompts dentro de un ámbito de estudio determinado; la pregunta de investigación es ¿existen diferencias estadísticamente significativas entre los factores que miden la calidad de respuesta y las herramientas IAG?; se contrasta hipótesis “no existen diferencias significativas”. La metodología consistió en seleccionar cinco herramientas de IAG; se solicitó que construyeran 100 prompts para integrar 500 prompts revisados por docentes de nivel superior en temas de estadística, probabilidad y sus aplicaciones; se recopilaron respuestas de esos prompts y sus autoevaluaciones con una rúbrica de 12 criterios. Con 500 evaluaciones, se realizó análisis factorial exploratorio (AFE) con 70% de datos; se prosiguió con un análisis factorial confirmatorio (AFC) con 30% de datos. El AFE arroja que es recomendable dos factores que definen el constructo de calidad y el AFC indica que la rúbrica si tiene condiciones de validez para medir la calidad. Se definen dos factores que miden el constructo “Solidez y consistencia técnica” y “Ejemplificación y aplicaciones ilustrativas”. El análisis descriptivo se hizo con una muestra al 99% de confianza, margen de error 1% y valor de proporción 5%. La confiabilidad de Cronbach fue de 0.93. Las pruebas de Kruskall Wallis y pruebas subsiguientes arrojan que si hay diferencias significativas principalmente en el factor “Solidez y consistencia técnica”.
authors: ['Rubén Pizarro Gurrola', 'José Gabriel Rodríguez Rivas', 'Araceli Soledad Domínguez Flores', 'Elda Rivera Saucedo']

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: Análisis del Impacto de la Inteligencia Artificial en Estudiantes Universitarios de Contaduría
abstract: Hoy en día la Inteligencia Artificial se ha presentado como una oportunidad y desafío para el ejercicio profesional de la Contaduría Pública por la automatización, precisión y rapidez con la que se pueden elaborar procedimientos administrativos y contables; esta investigación se realizó con 61 estudiantes de la Licenciatura en Contaduría de la Unidad Académica del Norte del Estado de Nayarit con el objetivo de identificar el nivel de conocimiento del uso de herramientas de Inteligencia Artificial con un muestreo estratificado que permitió identificar los niveles de conocimiento y uso de la misma, percibiendo que si conocen la Inteligencia Artificial pero se requiere mayor capacitación para los docentes y estudiantes para un adecuado uso y aprovechamiento de sus beneficios.

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: Viabilidad y Pertinencia de la Inteligencia Artificial en la Metodología Pedagógica Docente
abstract: Esta investigación analizó la viabilidad y pertinencia de la implementación de la Inteligencia Artificial en la herramienta pedagógica de los maestros de la IE Sor Juana Inés de la Cruz, Medellín-Colombia, de acuerdo a su contexto institucional, formativo y tecnológico. Se utilizó un paradigma de tipo interpretativo, con enfoque cualitativo, y se realizaron dos entrevistas: una a los directivos y coordinadores, con el fin de caracterizar el contexto institucional, formativo y tecnológico; y otra a profesores de distintas áreas para conocer las percepciones, actitudes y desafíos frente a la incorporación de IA. Los docentes afirman que la IA puede transformar el aprendizaje autónomo, la personalización educativa, la retroalimentación inmediata y la optimización del control académico. Sin embargo, existen obstáculos estructurales que limitan su viabilidad: infraestructura tecnológica insuficiente y obsoleta; formación docente esporádica sin enfoque específico en IA; falta de políticas institucionales; y cuestiones éticas de pertinencia sobre dependencia tecnológica, plagio y protección de datos. Se concluye que la incorporación efectiva de la IA requiere invertir estratégicamente en la infraestructura, en programas de formación permanente que integren competencias técnicas y éticas y en el desarrollo de marcos normativos institucionales que orienten su apropiación pedagógica contextualizada, que permita la equidad y la superación de las brechas digitales existentes.

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: Prototipo de una Herramienta Predictiva para la Selección de Obsequios en la Tienda de Regalos Dallas, basada en Inteligencia Artificial y Analítica de Datos.
abstract: El presente artículo tiene como objetivo presentar el desarrollo y evaluación de una herramienta predictiva basada en inteligencia artificial (IA) para la Tienda de Regalos DALLAS, con el fin de mejorar la experiencia de compra de los clientes y optimizar la gestión operativa. El proyecto responde a la problemática de la indecisión de los clientes al seleccionar regalos, lo que afecta tanto la satisfacción del comprador como la del destinatario. Para abordar este reto, se diseñó un prototipo que integra ChatGPT para el procesamiento de lenguaje natural y Microsoft Excel para el análisis de datos, generando recomendaciones personalizadas. La investigación se desarrolló mediante la metodología SCRUM, estructurada en sprints que permitieron una retroalimentación constante y un desarrollo ágil del prototipo. Un instrumento de opinión fue aplicado a 308 clientes, cuyos resultados revelaron que un 45% experimentaba incertidumbre al seleccionar regalos, y solo un 27% estaba satisfecho con los regalos recibidos. Tras la implementación del sistema predictivo, se logró una reducción del 30% en la indecisión, pasando del 45% al 15%, y un aumento significativo de la satisfacción del cliente, alcanzando un 60% de satisfacción. Estos hallazgos confirman que la IA puede ser una herramienta eficaz para mejorar la experiencia del cliente en el retail, optimizando la toma de decisiones y fortaleciendo la fidelización. Asimismo, la implementación del sistema permitió una gestión más eficiente del inventario, alineando la oferta con la demanda real (Grewal, Roggeveen & Nordfält, 2017).

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: Inteligencia Artificial y la Automatización de Procesos Contables y Fiscales: Implicaciones Éticas y Legales
abstract: La Inteligencia Artificial (IA) y la automatización han transformado los procesos contables y fiscales, optimizando la eficiencia y reduciendo errores humanos. Estas tecnologías permiten la detección de fraudes, la agilización del cumplimiento normativo y la automatización de tareas repetitivas. Sin embargo, su implementación conlleva riesgos éticos y legales, como la falta de transparencia en los algoritmos y la protección de datos sensibles. Además, la responsabilidad legal en decisiones automatizadas sigue siendo un desafío. La ingeniería aplicada a la automatización juega un papel clave en la integración segura y efectiva de estas tecnologías, requiriendo el desarrollo de sistemas robustos y la capacitación del personal.
authors: ['Ana Margarita Núñez Cortéz', 'Mario Alfredo Chaparro Hernández', 'Sandra Sital Muñoz', 'Gisela Haro Esquivel', 'Pablo Ayala Hernández']

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: Planificación Curricular 2.0: Estrategias Impulsadas por la Inteligencia Artificial para un Aprendizaje Efectivo en Entornos virtuales
abstract: La planificación curricular fundamenta su relevancia al garantizar una enseñanza eficaz y lograr los objetivos educativos, especialmente en entornos virtuales. Este estudio examina el impacto de la inteligencia artificial en la planificación curricular y cómo puede contribuir a un mejor aprendizaje en los entornos virtuales. Se empleó la revisión documental como principal técnica metodológica, recabando investigaciones relevantes publicadas entre 2019 y 2024 de las bases de datos académicas SCOPUS y Web of Science. Los hallazgos permiten discriminar que la IA posibilita la personalización del aprendizaje mediante algoritmos que adaptan contenidos y estrategias a las necesidades individuales de los estudiantes. Los asistentes virtuales mejoran la interacción y el acceso a recursos educativos y se identifican desafíos importantes, como la privacidad de datos y el sesgo algorítmico, que deben ser abordados para garantizar un uso ético y equitativo de la IA en la educación. Se concluye que la planificación curricular 2.0, potenciada por la inteligencia artificial, ofrece un modelo eficiente y flexible para mejorar la calidad del aprendizaje en entornos virtuales.

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: El Impacto Psicosocial de la Inteligencia Artificial en el País Mexicano
abstract: Este trabajo de investigación trata del impacto psicosocial que tiene la Inteligencia Artificial en el país Mexicano, realizada a través de un método experimental y cualitativo. Estamos en un proceso de transición, un cambio a nivel mundial donde no solo cambian las formas de pensar frente a los distintos sucesos sociales, sino también en las formas de interactuar y de comunicarse, esto a través de la tecnología y es que esta ha evolucionado, hablaremos del impacto que tiene a nivel psicosocial, específicamente de la inteligencia artificial a través de un enfoque psicoanalítico, en el contexto que se describe.

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: Terapias dirigidas por inteligencia artificial en la predicción y manejo de arritmias ventriculares: una revisión sistemática
abstract: El objetivo de esta revisión sistemática fue analizar el papel de las terapias dirigidas por inteligencia artificial (IA) en la predicción y el manejo de las arritmias ventriculares, explorando su efectividad clínica, precisión diagnóstica y los desafíos éticos asociados. La metodología se basó en la guía PRISMA, incluyendo la búsqueda de literatura científica en bases de datos como PubMed, Scopus, ScienceDirect y IEEE Xplore, seleccionando 25 estudios publicados entre 2018 y 2024. Se aplicaron criterios de inclusión y exclusión rigurosos, y se analizaron los datos mediante tres categorías analíticas: algoritmos de predicción, intervención clínica y dilemas éticos.Los hallazgos revelan que los algoritmos de aprendizaje profundo, especialmente las redes neuronales convolucionales (CNN) y recurrentes (RNN), han mostrado una alta precisión en la detección precoz de arritmias ventriculares, superando en muchos casos a métodos tradicionales. Las intervenciones clínicas basadas en IA han optimizado la toma de decisiones en tiempo real, mejorando los resultados en pacientes de alto riesgo. No obstante, se identificaron desafíos éticos relacionados con la transparencia algorítmica, la responsabilidad clínica y la equidad en el acceso a estas tecnologías. Se concluye que la IA representa una herramienta transformadora, aunque requiere una implementación ética y regulada para su adopción efectiva en la práctica médica.
authors: ['Paula Nicole Trujillo Jácome', 'Giovanna del Rocio Gallegos Medina', 'Nelly Patricia Ugsha Chugchilan', 'Jahel Alejandra Guevara Cabrera', 'Aylín Doménica Veloz González', 'Gabriela Alexandra Condo Ulloa']

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: Desarrollo de habilidades del siglo XXI mediante la integración de juegos gamificados con inteligencia artificial: Una revisión sistemática de la evidencia empírica en contextos educativos formales
abstract: La presente revisión sistemática aborda el papel de los juegos gamificados con inteligencia artificial (IA) en el desarrollo de las habilidades del siglo XXI, lo que sin duda da respuesta a una notoria brecha en la literatura pedagógica. El objetivo que se planteaba el estudio es examinar cómo pueden potenciar el pensamiento crítico, la colaboración, la creatividad y la alfabetización digital mediante la aplicación del modelo PRISMA (en total se han seleccionado 20 artículos, desde 2017 hasta 2025, a partir de las bases de datos Scopus, SciELO y literatura gris). Los hallazgos muestran que los entornos gamificados mediante IA consiguen mejorar la toma de decisiones (los artículos revisados tienen una efectividad del 85%), pero también promueven la interacción colaborativa (75% del clima grupal se ve mejorado) e incluso promueven la innovación a través de escenarios adaptativos. También se muestra un incremento del 70% en las competencias digitales éticas. La conclusión apunta a la potente transformación que pueden realizar estas tecnologías en cuanto a generar cambios en los modelos educativos, hasta el punto de promover el uso sistemático y en conjunto con diseños pedagógicos inclusivos. Este trabajo proporciona una base para la investigación futura, así como para la generación de políticas educativas basadas en la evidencia.
authors: ['Virginia Mariuxi Franco Alarcón', 'Sulay Yanela Alvarado Barzola', 'Cinthia Elizabeth Leon Vargas', 'Fanny Carmita Peña Barreto', 'Inicel Dual Álava Maza']

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: Inteligencia Artificial Generativa en la Educación Básica: Oportunidades y Consideraciones para el Diseño de Escenarios Interdisciplinarios
abstract: La inteligencia artificial generativa está modificando la educación básica, mejorando competencias cognitivas, facilitando aprendizajes interdisciplinarios y comenzando a presentar nuevas cuestiones éticas que deben ser atendidas. Para analizar el impacto de la IAG en el ámbito educativo, se llevó a cabo una revisión sistemática: se buscó información en Scopus, Web of Science y SciELO a lo largo del tiempo comprendido entre 2018 y 2024, obteniendo 28 estudios conforme al criterio PRISMA. Los hallazgos obtenidos demuestran que la IAG mejora sustancialmente las capacidades de escritura creativa, resolución de problemas y pensamiento crítico a través de la personalización del aprendizaje, pero también permite que se lleven a cabo diseños curriculares que integren las diferentes disciplinas. Sin embargo, también se identificaron riesgos, entre ellos se puede citar a las brechas digitales, el plagio o los sesgos algorítmicos, que exigen la elaboración de marcos éticos y formación docente en las aplicaciones educativas de la IAG. Las conclusiones señalan la necesidad de equilibrar innovación y equidad, proponen un modelo de implementación que suponga una transformación del rol del docente hacia la mediación pedagógica aumentada y avanza en la elaboración de políticas públicas para poder garantizar el acceso igualitario. El estudio aporta evidencias de cara a futuras investigaciones longitudinales y diseños de intervención educativa responsable de la IAG.
authors: ['Anabel Yéssica Proaño Sánchez', 'Marcelo Geovanny Ambuludi Abrigo', 'Fabián Rigoberto Román Laínez', 'Margeory Jesenia Córdova Vivanco', 'Adrian Ramón Guaycha Maza']

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: Diseño e Implementación de un Sistema Inteligente de Monitoreo y Prevención de Robos basado en Visión Artificial
abstract: Este estudio presenta el desarrollo de un sistema avanzado de vigilancia basado en visión artificial, diseñado para prevenir y monitorear robos en tiempo real en la farmacia PharmaTodo de Riobamba. El objetivo general fue integrar algoritmos avanzados de visión artificial con una aplicación móvil para mejorar la detección de amenazas y la gestión de alertas. Se emplearon dos metodologías principales: el algoritmo YOLO v5 para identificar armas de fuego cortas y cuchillos, y Mediapipe para analizar gestos corporales críticos. Además, se desarrolló una aplicación móvil con interfaz intuitiva, respaldada por Firestore para la transmisión segura y en tiempo real de notificaciones. Las pruebas en un entorno operativo demostraron resultados alentadores: el modelo para armas cortas alcanzó una precisión del 100% y una sensibilidad del 83%, mientras que el modelo para cuchillos logró una precisión del 95% y una sensibilidad del 80%. Estos hallazgos confirman que la combinación de visión artificial y tecnologías móviles representa una solución eficaz para mejorar la seguridad en entornos comerciales, transformando la vigilancia y prevención de robos con herramientas avanzadas y confiables.
authors: ['Jimmy Andres Villla Yuquilema', 'Edgar Giovanny Cuzco Silva']

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: Herramientas de Inteligencia Artificial y sus usos en la Innovación de la Educación Superior
abstract: La presente investigación tiene como objetivo establecer la percepción y uso del personal docente de la Universidad de Oriente en Valladolid, Yucatán, México con respecto a la inteligencia Artificial y su uso en la educación; para ello se abordó desde una perspectiva cuantitativa con alcance descriptivo, utilizando un cuestionario con opciones de respuesta cerrada y una pregunta abierta, el cual resultó de la previa revisión de la literatura sobre el tema, Que cada vez tiene más relevancia no sólo en la educación, sino en otros ámbitos en la vida de los seres humanos. Se encontró que sí conocen sobre herramientas de inteligencia artificial y que incluso pueden percatarse de cuando sus propios estudiantes la usan, incluso reconocieron haberla aplicado, sin embargo, el conocimiento que poseen es limitado y que desean tener capacitación, no sólo sobre su uso, sino sobre las implicaciones ética que la incorporación de la IA a sus actividades, y la de sus estudiantes, conlleva; es importante señalar que los participantes de la muestra tienen entre 30 y 50 años, lo que influye en el dominio que se tiene de la IA.
authors: ['Milton Carlos Berzunza Criollo', 'Suemy del Rosario Garrido Ayala', 'Naomy Aberaly Rivero Alcocer']

website: Centro de Investigación y Desarrollo E. Couture
title: Diseño e Implementación de un Sistema Automatizado para Llenado, Tapado y Separación de múltiples productos mediante el uso de Sistemas Neumáticos en conjunto con Visión Artificial
abstract: El sistema automatizado de dos bandas transportadoras proporciona una solución para el llenado, traslado y clasificación de contenedores de acuerdo a su color. A través del trabajo en conjunto de dos bandas transportadoras, una tolva y un sistema electroneumático, facilita el llenado automático, el transporte secuencial, la transición entre cintas, el cierre de contenedores y su clasificación por colores. El sistema se controla a través de un PLC Mitsubishi FX3U y un HMI Kinko GL070E, este sistema permite la vigilancia y modificaciones en tiempo real. El desarrollo abarcó el diseño eléctrico y neumático, la adquisición de componentes, la instalación del sistema de tapado, la clasificación por colores, el cableado, además, de la programación de HMI y PLC. Las pruebas realizadas arrojaron una tasa de éxito del 100% en la clasificación de contenedores con material de color azul y rojo, por otra parte, el color amarillo mostró una tasa del 95%.
authors: ['Juan Jesús Leyva-Noriega', 'Zayuri Pimentel-González', 'José Roberto Grande-Ramírez', 'Gabriel Antonio Navarrete-Hernández', 'Fernando Malagón-González']

title: Análisis de la Inteligencia Artificial: una perspectiva jurídica
abstract: La Inteligencia Artificial (IA) es una realidad y está presente en todos los aspectos cotidianos, hace un par de años se vislumbraba muy lejana, con cierta incertidumbre, posible sólo en ciencia ficción. Para dimensionar este fenómeno, basta con imaginar un mundo sin correo electrónico, teléfonos inteligentes, mensajería instantánea, redes sociales, videojuegos, autos autónomos, robots ni máquinas automatizadas, sin ellos prácticamente la sociedad retrocedería a la etapa moderna de principios del siglo XX. En el presente artículo se analiza el concepto de la Inteligencia Artificial, iniciando por su contexto actual, el uso y la forma como alcanza relevancia. Así mismo, se expone a través del método histórico una documentación de sus antecedentes, haciendo énfasis en los principales momentos que le dieron forma. Seguidamente, se utiliza el método analítico y exegético, por las consideraciones en aplicación de la IA en materia de instrumentos internacionales, con conceptos como Big Data (BD). Finalmente, se hace un breve análisis de las empresas transnacionales que utilizan sus sistemas compuestos por BD e IA. Se concluye con la visión progresiva y de trascendencia de los marcos normativos.
authors: ['Miguel Ángel Hernández Gómez', 'Fernando Guerrero Macedonio', 'Kevin Alexandro Gatica Tenangueño']

title: Adoption of e-learning in Zimbabwe’s higher education: Opportunities and challenges
abstract: This paper explores the adoption of e-learning in Zimbabwe's higher education, examining the opportunities and challenges associated with its implementation in the teaching and learning process. The paper was grounded in a systematic review approach, which provides a holistic view of the issue under investigation. In this regard, the PRISMA 2020 checklist ensured transparency and consistency in selecting the 60 papers from databases (Scopus, DOAJ, DHTE, WoS, Scielo SA, and IBSS). In addition, this approach ensured that all the crucial aspects of the issue under investigation were included in the analysis and discussion. The paper highlights e-learning as a concept and its process in the context of higher education. It also articulated a multifaceted approach that can be employed to enhance the adoption of e-learning in higher education. This led to the interrogation of the potential of e-learning to enhance accessibility, reduce costs, and improve educational outcomes. However, it also identifies significant barriers, including limited internet connectivity, inadequate technological infrastructure and resistance to change from both lecturers and students. In conclusion the adoption of e-learning in Zimbabwe's higher education presents both significant opportunities and notable challenges. To unlock the full potential of e-learning, policymakers, educational institutions and stakeholders need to collaborate in addressing these challenges. Ultimately, with the right approach, e-learning can play a transformative role in improving the quality and accessibility of higher education in Zimbabwe, contributing to broader educational socio-economic development targets.

title: The Use of Micro Video to Support Student Engagement in Online Learning
abstract: One of the challenges in online learning is the lack of student engagement in independent learning. The role of learning content significantly influences students' willingness to access and engage with the provided materials. This issue can be addressed through the use of microvideo learning content. Microvideos are short-duration video formats designed for efficient content delivery. This study aims to describe the use of microvideos in online learning and to analyze students' participation rates and learning completion. A qualitative descriptive research method was employed, involving 167 students enrolled in the Information and Communication Technology course. The findings reveal that student participation reached 90.42%, with a 100% learning completion rate. The use of microvideos in short-duration formats effectively reduces students' cognitive load.

title: The Impact of Blended Learning Models on Arab EFL Learners
abstract: This review examines the impact of blended learning models (BLMs), particularly flipped classrooms and online assignments, on Arab students learning English as a Foreign Language (EFL). It explores how these approaches influence motivation, engagement, and language proficiency within the unique educational and cultural context of the Arab world. The study employs a content analysis methodology, systematically analyzing empirical studies from reputable academic databases to identify key themes, patterns, and trends related to the effectiveness of BLMs. Through coding and thematic categorization, the analysis provides a structured understanding of how these models shape EFL learning outcomes. Theoretical foundations such as constructivism and connectivism underpin the study, emphasizing active learner engagement and digital access in the learning process. While BLMs offer benefits such as improved language skills and increased learner autonomy, challenges related to technological accessibility and cultural relevance persist. The review underscores the need for further research to refine and optimize BLM strategies for Arab EFL learners, focusing on overcoming barriers and adapting content to local educational needs

title: The Entrepreneurship, Socioeconomic Factors, and Motivation in Project-Based Learning Success: The Mediating Role of Self-Efficacy
abstract: Project-based learning has become one of the most favored learning methods in schools and universities. Therefore, it is important to examine how impactful this method is from the perspective of students' learning experiences. Project-based learning involves many students in interactive activities that affect them both individually and in groups. This research is a quantitative study aimed at examining the role of socio-economic factors, entrepreneurship, and student learning motivation on the success of project-based learning in the International Business course, mediated by the variable of self-efficacy. The analysis technique used is Structural Equation Modelling (SEM). The population consists of Management students who are taking courses using project-based learning. Data was collected through questionnaires distributed to 270 student respondents. The tool used was SEM-PLS. The findings of this study reveal three interesting points. First, learning motivation has a positive effect on self-efficacy. Second, self-efficacy influences the success of project-based learning. Third, student learning motivation impacts the success of project-based learning through the mediation of self-efficacy. This means that the higher the learning motivation, the greater its effect on learning outcomes. It is important for lecturers to provide motivational support to students in order to achieve learning objectives.

title: Analysis of the Need for Developing Teaching Books on Guidance and Counseling based on Project-Based Learning (PjBL) and Case Studies
abstract: The aim of this research is to analyze the need for developing teaching materials for Guidance and Learning Counseling (BKB) courses based on Project-Based Learning (PjBL) and case studies. This research uses a quantitative approach with a survey method. The research respondents were 188 students from education study programs who took the Guidance and Study Counseling course. Data was collected through a needs questionnaire that measured student assessments of various textbook components and learning methods. The data was analyzed using descriptive quantitative to find the average value for each item/statement regarding the components of the BK Belajar textbook material. The results of the quantitative analysis show that students have a high assessment of components that are relevant to the real world, chapter summaries, practice questions, and multimedia elements such as videos and visuals. Passive learning components such as “effective lectures” were rated low. These findings strongly validate the urgency of developing innovative PjBL-based textbooks and case studies to increase students' understanding, motivation and practical skills, while making a significant contribution to improving the quality of education in the field of guidance and counseling.

title: Chairing the Conversation: Learning Leadership Through Netflix’s "The Chair"
abstract: This iterative Scholarship of Teaching and Learning (SoTL) project explores how online graduate students experience a television show (Netflix’s The Chair) as a threaded case study in an asynchronous, eight-week organizational leadership course. Although implemented within a typically asynchronous program, the instructor assigned small groups to meet synchronously to discuss applying course content to the television series. Additionally, students wrote an organizational analysis paper on the fictitious Pembroke University and submitted two reflection assignments. Action research methodology (McNiff, 2013; Mertler, 2020) informed this SoTL project using quantitative and qualitative data collection and analysis tools. In total, 16 students over three course iterations participated; 11 completed the six-question survey, and student reflections generated 60 pages of text. The findings demonstrated that survey respondents met the intended course learning outcomes and positively rated the synchronous peer interaction. The qualitative text analysis of students’ reflections about The Chair and the synchronous discussion yielded four primary themes: (a) theory came to life, (b) connections to students’ personal experiences, (c) learning leadership vicariously, and (d) takeaways for students’ leadership practice. The findings highlight the transformative power of collaborative peer learning and the shared experience of a media-based threaded case study embedded within an online course.

title: Journeying into SoTL: A Transformative Experience Through the SPARK-ENG Professional Learning
abstract: In engineering education, the Scholarship of Teaching and Learning (SoTL) offers a pathway for educators to advance their professional learning and teaching practices. However, the perception of SoTL as less rigorous than traditional disciplinary research often discourages faculty engagement. Additionally, while engineering educators possess strong technical expertise in their respective fields, they often lack the knowledge, skills, and resources necessary for conducting educational research. These challenges were particularly evident among engineering educators at our university, a major Canadian institution. To address these gaps, we developed the SPARK-ENG program—a modular professional learning initiative providing support for engineering educators in their SoTL journey.

title: Serious games, student motivation, and emergent learning: Autoethnographic insights from an environmental studies classroom
abstract: ‘Serious’ games are growing in popularity as a teaching tool, but what are they teaching? In theory, games let us experience real-world issues and complications in a ‘safe’ space for learning, one where we can experiment with approaches and learn from mistakes. But an ontological tension is that the ‘unreality’ of games can encourage people to modify their behaviours in unexpected ways, impacting the kinds of learning that can take place. This essay uses an autoethnographic approach, drawing on structured vignette analysis to reflect on personal classroom experiences of teaching with games (Fishbanks and Climate Hazard Resilience Forums) in the field of environmental studies. Classroom experiences are contextualised using gamer motivation profiles as a theoretical lens, illustrating the ways in which different students interpret and enact ‘games’, which may yield unexpected outcomes and learnings. Games can offer a rich opportunity for students to experience negotiation and collaboration, and experiment with alternative environmental imaginaries. But along the way, as students interpret and enact the ‘roles and rules’ of games in different ways, potentially aligning with different gamer motivation profiles, learning through games can also force confrontations with difficult emotions and interpersonal dynamics. The essay concludes by reflecting on strategies that educators can use to mitigate such effects when teaching and learning with games.

title: UV-LEDs: Next-Generation Ultraviolet Light Sources
abstract: The development of ultraviolet (UV) light sources has evolved from traditional gas discharge sources (such as mercury and xenon lamps) to advanced solid-state semiconductor emitters. At the end of the twentieth century, breakthroughs in wide-bandgap semiconductor materials (including AlGaN, GaN, and AlN) led to the advent of ultraviolet light-emitting diodes (UV-LEDs). These LEDs offer advantages such as compact size, high energy efficiency, tunable wavelength, and environmental friendliness. They are widely used in water and air sterilization, photocatalytic degradation of pollutants, medical diagnostics, and biochemical sensing. However, achieving high external quantum efficiency (EQE) for deep ultraviolet C (UVC) LEDs remains challenging, including high dislocation density in epitaxial layers, low p-type doping efficiency in aluminum-rich alloys, insufficient light extraction due to total internal reflection, and reduced device lifetime and stability due to heat dissipation difficulties. In recent years, researchers have enhanced carrier injection and radiative recombination efficiencies by introducing superlattices and quantum well structures, improved light extraction efficiency (LEE) by employing nanopatterned substrates and photonic crystal structures, and mitigated device self-heating by utilizing highly thermally conductive substrates such as SiC and intrinsic AlN. Furthermore, advances in epitaxial technologies such as metal-organic chemical vapor deposition (MOCVD) and molecular beam epitaxy (MBE) have significantly reduced defect density and improved material uniformity. In the future, with further optimization of material quality, doping strategies, and optical cavity structures, deep ultraviolet LEDs are expected to rapidly replace traditional mercury lamps and become a highly efficient and sustainable ultraviolet light source solution for industrial, medical, and environmental applications.

website: Asosiasi Transformasi Digital dan Bisnis Indonesia
title: The Effect of Financial Literacy and Social Proof on Impulse Purchases of Virtual Items among Generation Z
abstract: This study analyzes the influence of Financial Literacy and Social Proof on Impulsive Buying behavior of virtual items among Generation Z users on the Roblox platform, with Usage Intensity as a mediating variable. Employing a quantitative approach, data were collected from 105 active Gen Z Roblox users in Indonesia through purposive sampling and analyzed using Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). The results reveal a unique phenomenon where Financial Literacy and Social Proof do not directly trigger impulsive buying. Instead, both variables significantly increase Usage Intensity. Subsequently, Usage Intensity acts as a full mediator that effectively bridges the influence of cognitive and social factors on impulsive buying decisions. These findings confirm a financial literacy paradox in the virtual economy, where high engagement and screen time (Usage Intensity) effectively weaken rational control, bridging the gap between cognitive knowledge and impulsive purchasing decisions.
authors: ['Juan Wody Romansa', 'Afidah Nur Aslamah', 'Osly Usman', 'Ryna Parlyna']

title: Development and Validation of an Augmented Reality-Based Career Card Game for Student Career Guidance
abstract: This study aims to develop and evaluate augmented reality-based career card games as a career information tool for high school students. The research method uses a modified Borg & Gall research and development model, with stages including needs analysis, product design, expert validation, and practicality testing. Data were obtained through questionnaires and interviews, then analyzed descriptively and quantitatively. The results showed that students need career guidance media that is more interesting, interactive, and technology-based. The AR Career Explorer product was declared highly valid and practical by experts, guidance counselors, and students. The novelty of this study lies in the integration of Holland's theory with AR technology in the form of a card game, which has not been found in previous studies. This media is suitable for use as an innovative alternative to improve career information services in high schools.

website: Asosiasi Transformasi Digital dan Bisnis Indonesia
title: The Effect of Feature Convenience and Discounts on Post-Purchase Feelings in TikTok Shop among Generation Z
abstract: The rapid growth of social commerce has transformed online shopping behavior, particularly among Generation Z consumers. TikTok Shop, as a social commerce platform, integrates interactive features, seamless purchasing processes, and promotional incentives such as discounts and flash sales. This study aims to analyze the impact of feature convenience and discounts on post-purchase feelings, specifically post-purchase satisfaction and post-purchase regret, among Generation Z TikTok Shop users. A quantitative research design was employed using a survey method, with data collected from respondents who had made purchases on TikTok Shop within the last six months. The data were analyzed using a partial least squares based approach. The results indicate that perceived ease of use has a significant positive effect on post-purchase satisfaction, while discounts do not show a significant direct influence on satisfaction. Furthermore, neither feature convenience nor discounts were found to have a significant effect on post-purchase regret. These findings suggest that post-purchase satisfaction in social commerce is driven more by platform usability and user experience than by promotional incentives. Meanwhile, post-purchase regret appears to be influenced by other factors beyond the scope of this study. The findings provide practical implications for social commerce platform developers to prioritize usability and for consumer education initiatives aimed at promoting responsible digital consumption among Generation Z.
authors: ['Alimah Alda Fuadiyah', 'Afidah Nur Aslamah', 'Osly Usman', 'Ryna Parlyna']

title: <b>Evaluación de insecticidas de nueva generación para el control de las cochinillas harinosas de las raíces del café, </b><b><i>Puto barberi </i></b><b>(Hemiptera: Putoidae) y</b><b><i> Dysmicoccus </i></b><b>sp. (Hemiptera: Pseudococcidae)</b>
abstract: In Colombia, mealybugs have become important common pests in coffee farming. The predominant species are Puto barberi (Hemiptera: Putoidae) and Dysmicoccus sp. (Hemiptera: Pseudococcidae). The objective of this research was to evaluate insecticides used to control these pests, which are not restricted for use in Colombia or coffee-buying countries. In the laboratory, 18 different formulations of insecticides, originating from mineral, biological, botanical, and chemical sources, were evaluated on both nymphs and adults of P. barberi. After 10 days of treatment, it was found that dinotefuran, pyriproxyfen + acetamiprid, cyantraniliprole, and a mixture of plant extracts from Cymbopogon citratus (DC) Stapf (Poales: Poaceae), Ammothamnus sp., and Pongamia pinnata (L.) Pierre (Fabales: Fabaceae) was effective in causing a mortality rate greater than 80 %. These products, along with spirotetramat, calcium hydroxide, and tiamentoxam (commercial control - CC), were then evaluated in a batch of naturally infested coffee plants by P. barberi and in coffee seedlings in a greenhouse that were artificially infested with Dysmicoccus sp. The study utilized a completely randomized design. The response variable was the number of live individuals per infested plant (LIPIP). On day 14 of treatment, only dinotefuran and the mixture of plant extracts showed statistically similar results to the CC, presenting 6.56 - 6.94 and 6.17 LIPIP for P. barberi. On days 30 and 60, Dysmicoccus sp. had 1.38 and 1.50 LIPIP with dinotefuran, statistically equal to 1.08 and 1.36 LIPIP of the CC; at 30 days, the mixture of plant extracts followed dinotefuran with 3.14 LIPIP. The effectiveness of these insecticides ranged from 73 % to 98 %; they are recommended for controlling coffee root mealybugs.
authors: ['Aníbal Arcila-Moreno', 'Zulma Nancy Gil-Palacio', 'Marisol Giraldo-Jaramillo', 'Rubén Medina', 'Pablo Benavides-Machado']

title: A Review On Perspectives Of Electrochemical Sensors In Next-Generation Healthcare
abstract: Impacts of sensors have increasing demand in various fields such asagriculture,environment,healthcare,medicine and pharmacology. Electrochemical approaches as a sole platform or integrated with metal organic frameworks/nanomaterials provide promising technologies due to their simplicity, high sensitivity and specificity. Electrochemical sensors have paved the way for emerging novel technologies in early stage diagnosis of diseases through its related biomarkers and biochemicals.Hence our primary focus is to review novel technologies for the early-stage detection and diagnosis of diseases (cancer,cardiovascular and diabetes ) related biomarkers such as alpha-fetoprotein, hemin, glucose, uric acid,hydrogen peroxide and nicotine. Our review aims to provide insights of integrated electrochemical sensors with metal organic frameworks (MOF), nanomaterials and microfluidics. The new era of digital health depends on various bioanalytical techniques through swift processes that produce reliable results.Hence this paper outlines and summarize the advancements in electrochemical approach that are applicable in healthcare and medicine...

title: Repetition, Retrieval, and Spaced Practice
abstract: Abstract Repetition, retrieval, and spaced practice refer to three linked methods for practicing a wide range of learning tasks, including those related to the learning of second languages (L2s). These methods have been interpreted and conceptualized broadly by researchers in recent years and have been used to practice a broad spectrum of activities, including communicative tasks. For example, repetition can refer to whether a learner repeats an activity or task after having completed it. Alternatively, it can refer to frequency effects, that is, how often individual constructions, such as vocabulary items, repeat in the input. Retrieval practice is a subtype of repetition practice that involves recalling previously learned information from memory. Spaced practice involves how the repetitions are distributed (e.g., one hour, one day, and one week between repetitions) and how the distribution influences learning and retention. This entry provides an overview of the theory and research into repetition, retrieval, and spaced practice.

website: Universitas Islam Negeri Syekh Ali Hasan Ahmad Addary Padangsidimpuan
title: The Effect Of Information Overload On Decision Postponement Mediated By Consumer Confusion  In Generation Z In Jepara
abstract: This study aims to examine the influence of information overload on decision postponement, mediated by consumer confusion. The research adopts a quantitative approach, targeting the Generation Z population in Jepara. Data collection was conducted using completed questionnaires distributed to respondents selected through random sampling techniques and the Slovin formula. A total of 310 samples were analyzed using the PLS-SEM method with Smart PLS version 4.0. The research findings indicate that excess information does not directly influence decision postponement. However, information overload positively and significantly affects consumer confusion, and consumer confusion, in turn, positively and significantly influences decision postponement. With the presence of consumer confusion as a mediating variable, the relationship between information overload and decision postponement demonstrates a positive and significant correlation. The variable of consumer confusion in this study successfully mediates the connection between information overload and decision postponement, thereby producing positive and significant effects.

title: GENDER AND INFORMATION LITERACY: EXPLORING EQUAL LEARNING OUTCOMES WITH AN AUGMENTED REALITY STRATEGY
abstract: This study investigates whether Augmented Reality Strategy (ARS) fosters equitable development of information literacy skills among male and female Nigerian secondary school students studying Financial Accounting. Anchored in an explanatory sequential mixed-methods design, the research employed a quasi-experimental framework and a 2×2×3 factorial design to examine the interaction effects of ARS, gender, and technology proficiency. Correspondingly, the null hypothesis posited that there would be no statistically significant difference in the mean information literacy scores of male and female students taught partnership and goodwill accounts using ARS. Quantitative data were collected via pre- and post-tests and analyzed using ANCOVA to control for covariates such as technology proficiency. Results indicate no statistically significant gender-based differences in post-intervention information literacy scores (F(1, 38) = 0.26, p = .612, η² = .007). As the p-value exceeds the conventional alpha threshold (.05), the null hypothesis is not rejected, providing empirical support for the conclusion that the Augmented Reality Strategy (ARS) facilitates comparable learning outcomes for male and female students. Qualitative interviews further illuminated how ARS enhances engagement, confidence, and conceptual clarity for both male and female learners. These findings affirm ARS as a gender-neutral strategy that democratises digital learning and reduces disparities in technology-mediated instruction. The study offers empirical support for inclusive innovation and practical guidance for policymakers and curriculum designers advancing gender equity in 21st-century skills.

title: The Awakening as Aesthetic Critique: “Art for Art’s Sake” and Autonomous Sensation in Kate Chopin’s Novel
abstract: ABSTRACT This article takes an interdisciplinary approach to understanding Kate Chopin’s aesthetics in The Awakening (1899), bringing in author biographical evidence, cultural artifacts, and Western aesthetic philosophy to illuminate the aspects of Chopin’s novel that tie her to European Aestheticism. It argues that Chopin not only participates in but progresses European Aestheticism primarily through Edna’s aesthetic and erotic experiences. Reading Chopin’s novel as an aesthetic critique, or philosophical contemplation of aesthetics, this article views The Awakening as a progression of Western aesthetic philosophy at large. It shows how the novel engages with concepts featured in aesthetic discourse from early philosophers Edmund Burke and Immanuel Kant to the nineteenth-century British Pre-Raphaelites and European Aesthetes. These transatlantic and interdisciplinary connections help Chopin meditate on the aesthetic problems of the ubiquity and inevitability of mediation and artifice—issues that Chopin believes should be distinguished from questions of morality. Understanding Chopin’s final novel as a progression of a white, male-authored European tradition of aesthetic philosophers and Aesthetes has the potential to alter existing notions of whose work constitutes the field of aesthetic theory.

website: Universitas Islam Negeri Syekh Ali Hasan Ahmad Addary Padangsidimpuan
title: Teachers as Agents of Religious Moderation:  A Study on the Use of Islamic Education Textbooks in Madrasah Aliyah
abstract: This study examines the textbooks and teachers of PAI (Islamic Religious Education) in the process of transmitting Islamic religious knowledge at Madrasah Aliyah, Manado City. Manado City was chosen as one of the tolerant cities in Indonesia where the majority of the population embraces Christianity, while Muslims are a minority. The focus of this research is teachers' perception of textbooks, religious patterns, and teachers' agency in learning Islamic religious subjects. Qualitative methods through in-depth interviews are used as instruments to csollect and analyze research data. The results of this study show that the 2013 PAI Curriculum textbook published by the Ministry of Religion of the Republic of Indonesia is too complex, but not in-depth, so that teachers need to innovate to find teaching resources from the internet. PAI teachers in the city of Manado reject the perception of Madrasah which has been considered as an institution that produces extreme ideas in religion. On the contrary, this study shows religious teachers as agents of religious moderation. Classrooms and teachers become mediators and facilitators for students who gain intolerant-exclusive religious understanding from offline and online recitations. Islamic religious learning in madrassas in the city of Manado is a mechanism to reduce radicalism by making the classroom an arena for discourse to develop religious moderation in the city of Manado.

website: International Forum of Researchers and Lecturers
title: Design of an Autonomous Solar Powered Smart Aquaculture Monitoring System for Energy Efficiency and Environmental Impact Reduction
abstract: Aquaculture is a vital sector in global food production, providing essential protein sources. However, the industry faces significant challenges, including high energy consumption and environmental impact. The integration of renewable energy, particularly solar power, with automation and IoT systems offers a promising solution to enhance energy efficiency, sustainability, and productivity in aquaculture operations. This study aims to evaluate the effectiveness of solar powered autonomous systems in reducing energy usage, improving operational efficiency, and promoting environmental sustainability in aquaculture. Literature Review: Recent research has explored various technologies, such as Digital Twins (DTs) and Precision Fish Farming (PFF), which integrate IoT sensors for real time monitoring and optimization of fish farming operations. The combination of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the Internet of Things (IoT), known as AIoT, has further advanced the industry by enabling automated decision making and predictive analytics. Solar power integration with IoT systems has been shown to significantly reduce operational costs, minimize carbon emissions, and enhance the sustainability of aquaculture practices. These advancements have the potential to address the challenges of energy consumption and environmental degradation in the industry. Materials and Method: This research utilizes a hybrid solar powered IoT system for aquaculture, integrating solar panels, IoT sensors, and automated control systems. The system monitors key water quality parameters, such as pH, dissolved oxygen, turbidity, and temperature, to maintain optimal conditions for aquatic life. Data is collected through IoT sensors and analyzed through a cloud-based platform. A pilot study is conducted on a small scale aquaculture farm to evaluate the system's performance, including energy consumption, water quality management, and fish health. Energy savings, operational efficiency, and environmental impact are assessed. Results and Discussion: The integration of solar powered IoT systems significantly reduced energy consumption compared to traditional systems, with a notable decrease in grid electricity reliance. The system successfully maintained optimal water quality conditions, enhancing fish health and growth. Solar powered systems proved reliable, even in regions with variable sunlight, and demonstrated improvements in operational efficiency through automation. The environmental benefits were evident, with a reduction in carbon emissions and lower operational costs. The study highlights the feasibility of solar powered IoT systems as a sustainable solution for modern aquaculture operations.
authors: ['Lukman Medriavin Silalahi', 'Safrizal Safrizal', 'Erick Fernando', 'Hayadi Hamuda', 'Ribut Julianto', 'Yuanita Sinatrya']

title: IMPLEMENTATION OF AUTONOMOUS POWER SUPPLY FOR LOWPOWER DEVICES OF THE INTERNET OF MEDICAL THINGS
abstract: Background. The paper aims at improving the autonomous power supply system for medical Internet of Things devices by introducing betavoltaic power sources. Materials and methods. The research method is based on the implementation of an experimental sample of a betavoltaic complex and testing its characteristics on a test bench. Results. The results of the work consist in the implementation of hardware and software components of the test bench (which is a debug board) designed to select the operating mode of betavoltaic assemblies included in the betavoltaic complex, testing it and formulating proposals for further use of the proposed approach for designing and hardware implementation of betavoltaic complexes as end devices providing pulse power supply for Internet of Medical Things devices. Conclusions. The conclusions are to substantiate the advantages of implementing the proposed approach, namely, using a betavoltaic complex as a power supply system for the Internet of Medical Things and further application of the obtained results in the healthcare system and medical engineering education.

title: UASI-PRECISE CONTROL OF AN AUTONOMOUS MOBILE ROBOT
abstract: Background. In modern production, optimization of production processes is becoming increasingly important. This article discusses the principles of path construction by an autonomous mobile robot, including in an environment with static and dynamic obstacles. Materials and methods. The research is based on the interaction of MES/APS systems (Manufacturing Execution System/Advanced Planning and Scheduling) in combination with IIoT (Industrial Internet of Things) and mobile robots. Results. An algorithm of relay control with an artificial dead zone and an algorithm of obstacle avoidance in a local environment based on quasi-fuzzy logic are proposed. Conclusions. The proposed intelligent control algorithms allow reducing the cost of a logistics project based on autonomous robots and abandoning a number of expensive software.
authors: ['Vitaly R. Aleksandrov', 'Nikita V. Azarnov', 'Aleksey A. Shchetkin', 'Aleksandr P. Kushnir', 'Roman M. Оgoreltcev']

title: DIGITAL FREQUENCY MULTIPLIERS IN AUTONOMOUS INFORMATION AND MEASUREMENT SYSTEMS
abstract: Background. Increasing the performance of remotely controlled on-board equipment of autonomous information and measurement systems when entering flight task data into it requires the use of a clock frequency significantly higher than that required for autonomous operation of equipment selected based on the minimum current consumption. The task to eliminate this contradiction, complicated by the need to have information about the exact ratio of the used frequencies, is urgent. Materials and methods. The solution of the above problem can be realized by using digital frequency multipliers. Results. The variants of frequency multipliers construction are considered. It is shown that the known variants of multipliers construction cannot fully satisfy the necessary requirements. Multipliers using a pulse pack generator have a large unevenness of the output pulses, and multipliers using automatic frequency adjustment do not have instantaneous mode output, moreover, multipliers with automatic tuning and a frequency detector on a 512PS8VC chip can have a duration of transients exceeding several tens of seconds. A combined method of frequency multiplication is proposed. Conclusions. It is shown that the combined method of frequency multiplication based on generation of pulse packs and auto-tuning with phase detection combines positive qualities of pulse packs generation in the part of instantaneous output to the mode and small non-uniformity of output pulses of auto-tuning.
